items.briefing:
A new phishing campaign is targeting WhatsApp users by sending fake business documents that, when opened, can compromise your computer. This is a direct risk for Australian staff who receive unexpected files through WhatsApp. Separately, two ransomware threats are active: the "Gentlemen" gang is providing tools to attackers that can disable security software, and a new ransomware strain called "Prinz Eugen" targets your most recently modified files first — meaning active work documents are at greatest risk.

If you receive an unexpected document via WhatsApp — even from a known contact — do not open it. Verify the sender through a separate channel before downloading anything.

featured.title: ACSC warns of reported credential exposure affecting Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways
featured.quote: Critical infrastructure vulnerability requires immediate patching of widely-deployed firewall and VPN devices.
